topic: AI Agent Market Size, Growth Projections & Competitive Landscape (2025-2026)
executive_summary: The global AI agents market is valued at ~$7.6B in 2025, projected to reach $10.9B in 2026 with a 43-50% CAGR. The market is expected to hit $47-53B by 2030 and $183-199B by 2033-2034. 85% of enterprises are implementing AI agents by end of 2025. Funding is explosive — AI startups attract 33% of total VC, with average deal size up 86% YoY to $49.3M. The market is dominated by hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Salesforce) at the enterprise tier, well-funded AI labs (OpenAI at ~$1T valuation, Anthropic at $183B) in the model/API layer, and a growing crop of agent-builder platforms (CrewAI, Relevance AI, Lindy, Taskade) competing on pricing and ease of use. Pricing is rapidly shifting from per-seat to credit/usage/outcome-based models.

adoption_stats.retail_investment_increase: 76% of retailers increasing AI agent investment
adoption_stats.marketing_roi: 80% of marketers say AI tools exceeded ROI expectations

pricing_trends.credit_based: 126% YoY growth — dominant emerging model. Adopted by Figma, HubSpot, Salesforce
pricing_trends.outcome_based: 30% of enterprise SaaS had outcome components in 2025, projected 40% by 2026 (Gartner)
pricing_trends.hybrid_models: 43% adoption, projected 61% by end of 2026. Platform fee + usage-based AI charges
pricing_trends.per_seat_decline: Feb 2026 SaaS crash erased $285B — AI reduces seat requirements
pricing_trends.ai_native_usage_based: 83% of AI-native SaaS companies use usage-based pricing

key_trends: Shift from per-seat to usage-based and outcome-based pricing
Enterprise adoption reaching 85% by end of 2025
Mega-rounds dominating funding ($100M+ deals = 75%+ of funding)
Asia Pacific fastest-growing region for AI agents
Vertical-specific agents emerging (healthcare, legal, finance, dev tools)
AI cost transparency becoming a competitive differentiator (BYO-LLM, pass-through pricing)
Unified command center / AI HQ category is underserved — most tools are point solutions
